title: 7th Angel Clockiel
wikitext:
[]: [Spoilers] 7th Angel ("Clockiel")             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Aug 11, 2009 9:10 pm Concerning Clocky, I'd say that his appearance wasn't what I expected but it was still cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva4Dummies » Sun Aug 23, 2009 2:17 pm Ok, i have a question for you all, you know when Asuka air kicks Clockiel there is a spike thing on the eva's foot, Did it come out of the eva or was it one of the crossbow gun's spikes stuck on the AT field. I personally think it is a new feature in the eva production model, but...

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Aug 23, 2009 2:36 pm Crossbow.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sun Aug 23, 2009 2:39 pm Eva4Dummies wrote: Ok, i have a question for you all, you know when Asuka air kicks Clockiel there is a spike thing on the eva's foot, Did it come out of the eva or was it one of the crossbow gun's spikes stuck on the AT field

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 23, 2009 4:53 pm Yes, it was one of the "arrows" from the crossbow.

[]:             Quote Post by cambaprecoz » Tue Aug 25, 2009 11:01 pm Reichu wrote: Yes, it was one of the "arrows" from the crossbow.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 31, 2010 7:05 am Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion Posted: Fri May 28, 2010 8:41 pm ( Original post location ) Here's a few pics for Clockiel. "Hi there, you must die now." And the battleships go boom. Talk about bloody murder. "Oh sh*t, a German Gunbuster wannabe! I better defend myself." _______________________________________________________ Reichu The last shot provides a distant view of Clockiel's cruciform head transformation, in case anyone was wondering what the heck was going on with that first closeup of its head (ticktock, ticktock, BOOM!). I'll add some HD shots later if someone doesn't beat me to it. (Reichu = OCD = has to label everything by cut number.) ________________________________________________________ Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion Posted: Mon May 31, 2010 11:57 am ( Original post location ) Anyone think Othniel's a hydrokinetic? I mean, he freezes water and forms his cross beams from them so....

[]:             Quote Post by Delular » Tue Jun 01, 2010 6:34 am Aren't the projectiles shot out of crossbows called 'bolts'? Too much Dungeons and Dragons :S @Reichu [code:1]Yes, it was one of the "arrows" from the crossbow.[/code:1] I am not speaking about the angels head, just mentioning that bolts are shot out of crossbows as arrows are shot from bows. A poor D&D joke =(

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 01, 2010 10:00 am Delular wrote: Aren't the projectiles shot out of crossbows called 'bolts'?

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Fri Jun 04, 2010 5:51 pm Just in case. Eva-02 was shooting from uberelektromagnetischen armbrust or superelectromagnetic crossbow. (Sounds banally, huh?)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jun 04, 2010 6:54 pm Delular wrote: Aren't the projectiles shot out of crossbows called 'bolts'?

[]:             Quote Post by Brutalman » Mon Jul 05, 2010 9:57 pm From those images: the basic structure of Clockiel, and the way it destroys 10 of the battleships at once and fires 9 or 10 of those black beams/arms at Unit 02 - it gives me the feeling that it's meant to be another Tree of Life reference. I can't reconcile the various knobs on the structure with the 10 sephiroth though.

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Mon Jul 05, 2010 11:13 pm NemZ wrote: Yes, it's really that stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Tycho » Mon Jul 05, 2010 11:18 pm bah, this is just Matariel 2.0. No wonder it was killed by a few bolts.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Jul 06, 2010 2:06 am NemZ wrote: She actually fired SEVERAL bolts, all perfectly on target and stacked up in a single tall pile end-to-end (yes, while still freefalling). When she does the kick she drives the last bolt through the rest of the stack, splitting each below it on the way in. Yes, it's really that stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 06, 2010 2:10 am SaltyJoe wrote: Up until then, the whole affair felt like a smooth, professional military incursion (Itano-circus included)

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Tue Jul 06, 2010 3:20 am I liked it. It made me laugh in a good way. What else makes me laugh is questioning the reality of Asuka's acrobatics given the fact that she's tucked inside a 200 ft tall monster possessed by her dead mother.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Jul 06, 2010 3:26 am The whole 'Rider-kick' sequence is no more stupid than some of the things we've seen in the series. 'Dance like you want to win' anyone? I just think they were trying to keep a lighter mood early on in the film, before shit got real.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Tue Jul 06, 2010 4:35 am So what is Clockiel the angel of; is he Salgiel, the angel of snow/ice; is he Eth, angel of time; is he Attarib, angel of winter; is he Sameron, angel of the twelfth hour of the day? Perhaps Baradiel, angel of hale?

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Tue Jul 06, 2010 8:24 am Perhaps Baradiel, angel of hale?
